A strand of silver light tightens around drifting wreckage as the party passes. The snag is not alive, but the hungry shadow riding it lunges the moment anyone reaches for the cord.
A narrow astral skiff slides out of a colorless cloud with weapons already drawn. Its crew seems more interested in maps and memories than coin.
A shattered battle-memory hangs in the air as glittering steel fragments. When disturbed, the fragments arrange themselves into darting blades and strike at the nearest mind.
A faint color pool opens and closes between slow pulses of light. Reading its hue correctly reveals what plane it leans toward before the surface folds away.
Broken cups, splinters, and old coins hang motionless until the party crosses their invisible orbit. One careless push sends the whole field spinning like a storm of needles.
For a few breaths, all sound fades and the stars seem close enough to drink from. Resting in the stillness clears pain from old bruises and steadies frayed nerves.
A repeating memory asks for a name it no longer remembers. Following the echo could lead to a vanished traveler, a discarded secret, or the mind that abandoned it.
A brass navigation buoy flashes a distress pattern no current chart recognizes. Repairing it may reveal a hidden route before another crew arrives to claim the salvage.
A distant silver cord vibrates like a plucked string, carrying fear without words. Reaching the source means crossing open astral space before the signal stops.
Clear crystals bud from a dead memory like fruit from a branch. They dissolve on the tongue unless wrapped before the next surge of astral wind.
Ribbon-like growths trail from a drifting stone, folding toward strong emotions. Cutting only the pale tips leaves the living fringe intact for the next traveler.
A low hollow is dusted with fine salt left behind by evaporated dreams. A steady hand can collect the bright grains before a stray thought scatters them.
Tiny fish swim through empty air in a tight knot around a mote of gravity. They vanish if startled, leaving only ripples in the dark.
A translucent eel loops through a ring of blue light, biting at anything that breaks the circle. Catching it takes quick hands and careful timing.
A shell the size of a helm opens whenever someone thinks about home. The pearl inside is real, but the clam snaps shut on fear.
Mineral crust has formed around the edge of a fading color pool. The flakes hold a weak planar charge until exposed to ordinary rain.
A spray of dark iron pebbles drifts in a slow spiral around an old impact scar. Gathering them means matching the spiral without sending the stones away.
Fine silver dust glitters in a crack across a floating stone. Too much force breaks the stone apart and wastes the best of the seam.
A broken spar from an old spelljamming wreck turns slowly end over end. It is still sound if cut loose without cracking the enchanted grain.
A small grove of dead dreamwood floats root-up in the void. Each pale branch is light as cork but splinters if handled without patience.
A mast marked with unfamiliar constellations drifts beside torn sailcloth. Freeing a usable length requires leverage before the wreckage tumbles away.
A dead nautiloid hull drifts with no lights, but disciplined thoughts move behind the torn crystal. The watchers want living memories more than salvage.
A vast shadow eclipses the starlight for one impossible heartbeat. The creature itself is distant, but the scavengers fleeing its wake strike at anything in their path.
A splinter of thought-storm bursts against the party and condenses into screaming motes. Each mote cuts with remembered pain before it burns out.
A ribbon of pale blue light points toward Toril when viewed from the right angle. Reading the drift before it folds away can shorten a long void crossing.
A silver tide rolls through the void and tries to pull names, debts, and faces loose from the mind. Holding onto one anchor thought keeps the party together.
A sealed case of spell-scrip tumbles beside a wrecked longboat. Its sigils remain valid in several ports if claimed before the ink forgets its value.
A brass bell rings without sound beside a shattered training buoy. Its pattern marks a cadet drill gone wrong and points deeper into astral weather.
Five crystal chart-slivers orbit one another like anxious moons. Reuniting them reveals the route a missing spelljamming crew tried to hide.
A practice skiff drifts with its helm cold and its logbook overwritten in a stranger's hand. The last entry begs any finder not to trust the rescue lights.
A small star moth circles an empty helm, responding only to calm voices and old academy signals. Bringing it home means crossing open void before raiders notice.
A widening color pool shows a ruined dock on Toril, but its edge is already sealing. Someone on the far side is holding up a lantern in distress.
Hard translucent seeds drift from a thought-grown vine. They pulse when named aloud and spoil if handled by a fearful mind.
A honeycomb of golden light hangs inside an abandoned cabin bell. Each cell tastes faintly of a different summer on Toril.
Mint-bright sprigs grow on a stone that has never known soil. Cutting them cleanly keeps their chill useful for spellwork and tea alike.
Flat luminous rays drift through a slow current of remembered sea. They scatter when anyone speaks a lie nearby.
Minnows flash around a pebble-sized gravity well, each turn faster than the last. A net must match the orbit before the shoal vanishes.
A thumb-sized squid glows inside a bubble of blue-black air. Its ink stains maps with temporary constellations.
Cold iron nails from an old spelljammer repair locker spin in a perfect ring. Gathering them safely means breaking the ring without waking its ward.
Tiny beads of starmetal drift like dew around a cracked helm socket. Each bead rings softly when moved toward Toril.
Amber has formed in the crack of a floating stone, trapping motes from a dozen planes. It flakes apart unless cut along the color bands.
A helmwood brace from a wreck still hums with stored direction. Salvaging it whole requires careful leverage before the wreck rotates away.
Pale mast splinters gather at the edge of a dream current. They are feather-light, useful, and quick to drift through careless fingers.
A rope-like vine coils around a dead spar and tightens when touched. Calm, steady cuts can turn it into strong cordage.
